sections.label: Section 724
sections.original: καλῶς ἀναγκαίως τε · συνενέγκοι δʼ ὅμως .
sections.translation: Finely here ! but still I must ; [*] good come of it for all that !

sections.label: Section 725
sections.original: οἶσθʼ οὖν ὃ δρᾶσον , ὦ γύναι ; πιθοῦ δέ μοι .
sections.translation: Do you know what to do , lady ? Then obey me .

sections.label: Section 726
sections.original: τί χρῆμα ; πείθεσθαι γὰρ εἴθισμαι σέθεν .
sections.translation: In what matter ? for I was ever accustomed to obey you .

sections.label: Section 727
sections.original: ἡμεῖς μὲν ἐνθάδʼ , οὗπέρ ἐσθʼ ὁ νυμφίος —
sections.translation: Here , where the bridegroom is , I will —

sections.label: Section 728
sections.original: μητρὸς τί χωρὶς δράσεθʼ , ἃ ἐμὲ δρᾶν χρεών ;
sections.translation: Which of my duties will you perform in the mother ’ s absence ?

sections.label: Section 729
sections.original: ἐκδώσομεν σὴν παῖδα Δαναϊδῶν μέτα .
sections.translation: Give your child away with help of Danaids .

sections.label: Section 730
sections.original: ἡμᾶς δὲ ποῦ χρὴ τηνικαῦτα τυγχάνειν ;
sections.translation: And where am I to be then ?

sections.label: Section 731
sections.original: χώρει πρὸς Ἄργος παρθένους τε τημέλει .
sections.translation: Go to Argos , and take care of your unwedded daughters .

sections.label: Section 732
sections.original: λιποῦσα παῖδα ; τίς δʼ ἀνασχήσει φλόγα ;
sections.translation: And leave my child ? Then who will raise her bridal torch ?

sections.label: Section 733
sections.original: ἐγὼ παρέξω φῶς ὃ νυμφίοις πρέπει .
sections.translation: I will provide the proper wedding torch .

sections.label: Section 734
sections.original: οὐχ ὁ νόμος οὗτος οὐδὲ φαῦλʼ ἡγητέα .
sections.translation: That is not the custom ; but you think lightly of these things .

sections.label: Section 735
sections.original: οὐ καλὸν ἐν ὄχλῳ σʼ ἐξομιλεῖσθαι στρατοῦ .
sections.translation: It is not good for you to be alone among a soldier-crowd .

sections.label: Section 736
sections.original: καλὸν τεκοῦσαν τἀμά μʼ ἐκδοῦναι τέκνα .
sections.translation: It is good that a mother should give her own child away .

sections.label: Section 737
sections.original: καὶ τάς γʼ ἐν οἴκῳ μὴ μόνας εἶναι κόρας .
sections.translation: Yes , and that those maidens at home should not be left alone .

sections.label: Section 738
sections.original: ὀχυροῖσι παρθενῶσι φρουροῦνται καλῶς .
sections.translation: They are well guarded in their maiden bowers .

sections.label: Section 739
sections.original: πιθοῦ .
sections.translation: Obey .

sections.label: Section 730b
sections.original: μὰ τὴν ἄνασσαν Ἀργείαν θεάν .
sections.translation: No , by the goddess-queen of Argos !

sections.label: Section 740
sections.original: ἐλθὼν δὲ τἄξω πρᾶσσε , τἀν δόμοις δʼ ἐγώ ,
sections.translation: Go , manage matters out of doors ; but in the house it is my place to decide what is proper for maidens at their wedding . [*]

sections.label: Section 742
sections.original: οἴμοι · μάτην ᾖξʼ , ἐλπίδος δʼ ἀπεσφάλην ,
sections.translation: Woe is me ! my efforts are baffled ; I am disappointed in my hope , anxious as I was to get my wife out of sight ; foiled at every point ,

sections.label: Section 745
sections.original: τέχνας πορίζω , πανταχῇ νικώμενος .
sections.translation: I form my plots and subtle schemes against my best-beloved . But I will go , in spite of all , with Calchas the priest , to inquire the goddess ’ s good pleasure , fraught with ill-luck as it is to me , and with trouble to Hellas . [*] He who is wise should keep in his house

sections.label: Section 750
sections.original: γυναῖκα χρηστὴν κἀγαθήν , ἢ μὴ τρέφειν .
sections.translation: a good and useful wife or none at all .

sections.label: Section 1.751
sections.original: ἥξει δὴ Σιμόεντα καὶ
sections.translation: The Hellenes ’ gathered army will come in arms aboard their ships to Simois with its silver eddies ,

sections.label: Section 1.755
sections.original: Ἴλιον ἐς τὸ Τροίας
sections.translation: to Ilium , the plain of Troy beloved by Phoebus ; where Cassandra , I am told , wildly tosses her golden tresses , wreathed with crown of green laurel ,

sections.label: Section 1.760
sections.original: κοσμηθεῖσαν , ὅταν θεοῦ
sections.translation: whenever the god ’ s resistless prophecies inspire her .

sections.label: Section 1.762
sections.original: στάσονται δʼ ἐπὶ περγάμων
sections.translation: And on the towers of Troy and round her walls shall Trojans stand , when sea-borne troops

sections.label: Section 1.765
sections.original: πόντιος εὐπρῴροιο πλάτας
sections.translation: with brazen shields row in on shapely ships to the channels of the Simois , eager to take Helen , the sister of that heavenly pair whom Zeus begot , from Priam , and bear her back to Hellas by toil

sections.label: Section 1.770
sections.original: ἐκ Πριάμου κομίσαι θέλων
sections.translation: of Achaean shields and spears .

sections.label: Section 773
sections.original: Πέργαμον δὲ Φρυγῶν πόλιν
sections.translation: The son of Atreus , encircling Pergamus , the Phrygians ’ town , with murderous war

sections.label: Section 775
sections.original: κυκλώσας Ἄρει φονίῳ ,
sections.translation: around her stone-built towers , dragging Paris ’ s head backward to cut his throat and sacking the city [*] from roof to base , shall be a cause of many tears to maids and

sections.label: Section 780
sections.original: δάμαρτά τε Πριάμου .
sections.translation: Priam ’ s wife . And Helen , the daughter of Zeus , shall [*] weep in bitter grief because she left her lord .

sections.label: Section 784
sections.original: μήτʼ ἐμοὶ
sections.translation: Never may there appear to me or to my children ’ s children

sections.label: Section 785
sections.original: ἐλπὶς ἅδε ποτʼ ἔλθοι ,
sections.translation: the prospect which the wealthy Lydian ladies and Phrygia ’ s brides will have [*] as at their looms they converse :

sections.label: Section 790
sections.original: Τίς ἄρα μʼ εὐπλοκάμου κόμας
sections.translation: Tell me , who will pluck me away from my ruined country , tightening his grasp on lovely tresses till the tears flow ? it is all through you , the offspring of the long-necked swan ; if indeed it is a true report

sections.label: Section 795
sections.original: ἔτυχεν , Λήδα ὄρνιθι πταμένῳ
sections.translation: that Leda bore [*] you to a winged bird , when Zeus transformed himself there , or whether , in the tablets of the poets , fables have carried these tales to men ’ s ears

sections.label: Section 800
sections.original: ἤνεγκαν παρὰ καιρὸν ἄλλως .
sections.translation: idly , out of season .

sections.label: Section 801
sections.original: ποῦ τῶν Ἀχαιῶν ἐνθάδʼ ὁ στρατηλάτης ;
sections.translation: Where is Achaea ’ s general ? Which of his servants will announce to him that Achilles , the son of Peleus , is at his gates seeking him ? For this delay at the Euripus is not the same for all of us ;

sections.label: Section 805
sections.original: οἳ μὲν γὰρ ἡμῶν , ὄντες ἄζυγες γάμων ,
sections.translation: there are some , for instance , who , bing still unwed , have left their houses desolate and are idling here upon the beach , while others are married but without children ; [*] so strange the longing for this expedition that has fallen on their hearts by the will of the gods . [*]

sections.label: Section 810
sections.original: τοὐμὸν μὲν οὖν δίκαιον ἐμὲ λέγειν χρεών ,
sections.translation: My own just plea I must declare , and whoever else has any wish will speak for himself . Though I have left Pharsalia , and Peleus , still I linger [*] here by reason of these light breezes at the Euripus , restraining my Myrmidons , while they are always pressing on me ,

sections.label: Section 815
sections.original: λέγουσʼ · Ἀχιλλεῦ , τί μένομεν ; πόσον χρόνον
sections.translation: saying : Why do we tarry , Achilles ? how much longer must we count the days to the start for Ilium ? do something if you are so minded ; or lead home your men , and do not wait for the tardy action of these Atridae .

sections.label: Section 819
sections.original: ὦ παῖ θεᾶς Νηρῇδος , ἔνδοθεν λόγων
sections.translation: Hail to you , son of the Nereid goddess ! I heard your voice

sections.label: Section 820
sections.original: τῶν σῶν ἀκούσασʼ ἐξέβην πρὸ δωμάτων .
sections.translation: from within the tent and came forth .

sections.label: Section 821
sections.original: ὦ πότνιʼ αἰδώς , τήνδε τίνα λεύσσω ποτὲ
sections.translation: O modesty revered ! who can this lady be whom I behold , so richly dowered with beauty ’ s gifts ?

sections.label: Section 823
sections.original: οὐ θαῦμά σʼ ἡμᾶς ἀγνοεῖν , οἷς μὴ πάρος
sections.translation: No wonder you do not know me , seeing I am one you have never before set eyes on ; I praise your reverent address to modesty .

sections.label: Section 825
sections.original: τίς δʼ εἶ ; τί δʼ ἦλθες Δαναϊδῶν ἐς σύλλογον ,
sections.translation: Who are you , and why have you come to the mustering of the Danaids — you , a woman , to a fenced camp of men ?

sections.label: Section 827
sections.original: Λήδας μέν εἰμι παῖς , Κλυταιμήστρα δέ μοι
sections.translation: I am the daughter of Leda ; my name is Clytemnestra ; and my husband king Agamemnon .

sections.label: Section 829
sections.original: καλῶς ἔλεξας ἐν βραχεῖ τὰ καίρια .
sections.translation: Well and shortly answered on all important points ,

sections.label: Section 830
sections.original: αἰσχρὸν δέ μοι γυναιξὶ συμβάλλειν λόγους .
sections.translation: but it is shameful for me to stand talking to women .

sections.label: Section 831
sections.original: μεῖνον — τί φεύγεις ; — δεξιάν τʼ ἐμῇ χερὶ
sections.translation: Stay ; why seek to escape ? give me your hand , a prelude to a happy marriage .

sections.label: Section 833
sections.original: τί φῄς ; ἐγώ σοι δεξιάν ; αἰδοίμεθʼ ἂν
sections.translation: What is it you say ? I give you my hand ? To lay a finger where I have no right , I could never meet Agamemnon ’ s eye .

sections.label: Section 835
sections.original: θέμις μάλιστα , τὴν ἐμὴν ἐπεὶ γαμεῖς
sections.translation: The best of rights you have , seeing it is my child you will wed , O son of the sea-goddess , daughter of Nereus .

sections.label: Section 837
sections.original: ποίους γάμους φῄς ; ἀφασία μʼ ἔχει , γύναι ·
sections.translation: What wedding do you speak of ? Words fail me , lady ; can your wits have gone astray and are you inventing this ?

sections.label: Section 839
sections.original: πᾶσιν τόδʼ ἐμπέφυκεν , αἰδεῖσθαι φίλους
sections.translation: All men are naturally shy in the presence of new relations ,

sections.label: Section 840
sections.original: καινοὺς ὁρῶσι καὶ γάμου μεμνημένοις .
sections.translation: when these remind [*] them of their wedding .

sections.label: Section 841
sections.original: οὐπώποτʼ ἐμνήστευσα παῖδα σήν , γύναι ,
sections.translation: Lady , I have never courted your daughter , nor have the sons of Atreus ever mentioned marriage to me .

sections.label: Section 843
sections.original: τί δῆτʼ ἂν εἴη ; σὺ πάλιν αὖ λόγους ἐμοὺς
sections.translation: What can it mean ? Your turn now to marvel at my words , for yours are very strange to me .

sections.label: Section 845
sections.original: εἴκαζε · κοινόν ἐστιν εἰκάζειν τάδε ·
sections.translation: Hazard a guess ; that we can both do in this matter ; for it may be we are both correct in our statements . [*]

sections.label: Section 847
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἦ πέπονθα δεινά ; μνηστεύω γάμους
sections.translation: What ! have I suffered such indignity ? The marriage I am courting has no reality it seems ; I am ashmed of it .

sections.label: Section 849
sections.original: ἴσως ἐκερτόμησε κἀμὲ καὶ σέ τις .
sections.translation: Some one perhaps has made a mock of you and me ;

sections.label: Section 850
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἀμελίᾳ δὸς αὐτὰ καὶ φαύλως φέρε .
sections.translation: pay no heed to it ; make light of it .

sections.label: Section 851
sections.original: χαῖρʼ · οὐ γὰρ ὀρθοῖς ὄμμασίν σʼ ἔτʼ εἰσορῶ ,
sections.translation: Farewell ; I can no longer face you with unfaltering eyes , after being made a liar and suffering undeservedly .
